Patch Set 4: Code-Review-1

(1 comment)

This patch is repeatedly failing the conf-sanity test_86 that was added by this patch.  It looks like the OST is not being started properly:

 06:02:06:LustreError: 137-5: lustre-OST0001_UUID: not available for connect from 10.2.4.140@tcp (no target). If you are running an HA pair check that the target is mounted on the other server.

Patch Set 4:

I think the conf-sanity test_86a failure comes from target state left by the previous test: test_84.

The Lustre client and the MDT0000 try to connect to OST0001 but OST0001 has simply not been started. The 'setup' only starts OST0000.

Could it come from the filesystem configuration stored by the MGS that has not been updated to reflect that OST0001 is stopped ?

Patch Set 4:

It seems that a "reformat" needs to be added at the end of test_84 to remove the old config.  This needs to be added in test_84 instead of test_85 or test_86 so that it is always run after that test finishes, rather whatever test is run next.

Hi Grégoire,

Could you please add the following Lustre version check codes into the sub-tests that will hit interop issue with old servers that do not contain this patch? Thank you.

        [[ $(lustre_version_code $SINGLEMDS) -ge $(version_code 2.7.56) ]] ||
                { skip "Need MDS version at least 2.7.56"; return 0; }

Patch Set 14:

> conf-sanity test 84 and 87 changes look really-really unrelated to the rest of this patch
These changes are necessary to leave the file system in a correct state for the next test. The issues were discovered when conf-sanity test 90 was following test 84 or test 87 in a previous patchset version.
I am going to move them to a separate patch.

parents.subject: LU-6480 o2iblnd: leak cmid in kiblnd_dev_need_failover

commit.subject: LU-5319 tests: testcases for multiple modify RPCs feature
commit.message:
LU-5319 tests: testcases for multiple modify RPCs feature

This patch creates new testcases in the Auster test suite to
test the support of multiple modify RPCs in flight feature.

Two new OBD_FAIL codes are added to allow several failure occurences
of the reint requests or replies. This is needed because the current
fail checks impose the OBD_FAIL_ONCE flag.

Added testcases are :
- sanity         245
- conf-sanity    90a 90b 90c 90d
- replay-single  102a 102b 102c 102d

This patch also removes replay-single tests 53a and 53b
from the ALWAYS_EXCEPT list.

Signed-off-by: Gregoire Pichon <gregoire.pichon@bull.net>
Change-Id: I3cd233d5ef67a696e830c77b154dcc01520bb3d2
Reviewed-on: http://review.whamcloud.com/14861
Tested-by: Jenkins
Tested-by: Maloo <hpdd-maloo@intel.com>
Reviewed-by: Jian Yu <jian.yu@intel.com>
Reviewed-by: Andreas Dilger <andreas.dilger@intel.com>
